reMarkable

Meet Norway’s first astronaut.

Jannicke Mikkelsen made history this year as commander of the historic Fram2 mission, traveling in true polar orbit.

Most days, she calls Svalbard home — an archipelago in the Arctic Circle — where she uses the harsh polar conditions as a testing ground for cinema technology, exploration, and survival.

Always next to her, on Earth and in space: remarkable Paper Pro.

#remarkable #remarkablepaperpro #papertablet #remarkablepaper #betterthinking

Can picking up a pen benefit your brain?

#Drawing on decades of research neuroscientist Dr. Audrey van der Meer looks at why handwriting is so important for learning and memory, particularly in children. Watch her full talk to see why handwriting deserves a spot in the classroom via our link in bio.

Been using the @remarkable Paper Pro, and it just makes everything feel easier

Feels like writing on paper, but without the clutter. Notes, sketches, documents—everything stays in one place, neatly organized

The color display adds just enough to highlight ideas without distractions, and the pen? Honestly, it feels natural. No lag, no weird digital feel—just smooth and responsive

No eye strain, no blue light, and no notifications popping up while you work. It’s just you and your ideas

reMarkable
Sponsored
Buttons have become glass touchscreens. What was once colorful is now gray.

Amber Case, founder of the Calm Tech Institute, says we’re losing dimensionality in our environment. Modern technology is making everything flatter, blander, and yet somehow, more demanding.

So how can we preserve those precious tangible, textured experiences, and make better tools?

To find out what “calm technology” is all about, watch our talk via the link in bio.

#reMarkablepaper #reMarkablePaperPro #papertablet #paperpro #betterthinking #calmtech #AmberCase
